rationale: Both correctly capture the two things the thread actually settled — stage/replica attribution already covered by the relabeling work, and tail_waste/fragmentation dropped as expected block-rounding overhead — plus the LLM-first scoping. X is better grounded with consistent cross-repo file/line citations and treats the make_stats throttle as the one real open item worth filing separately rather than dismissing it, though its 'closing the whole RFC' disposition overreaches since footprint/prefix-cache metrics were never explicitly resolved in the thread. Y's resolved/open breakdown is a reasonable structure, but its confident, thread-unsupported claim that the throttle is 'per-scheduler not global' and already non-issue post-#3576 risks waving away the reporter's own measured evidence without real grounding.
